questions.question: Given segment table: Seg0(Base=400,Limit=1200), Seg1(Base=2000,Limit=500), Seg2(Base=3000,Limit=800). Translate: (0,876), (1,500), (2,700).
How does segmentation support memory protection?
questions.answer: Each segment has protection bits (R/W/X). Code: R-X (read, execute). Data: RW- (read, write). Stack: RW-. Hardware checks permissions on each access. Violation causes trap. Also, limit checking prevents access beyond segment bounds (segmentation fault).

questions.question: What is external fragmentation in segmentation? How can it be solved?
questions.answer: External fragmentation: Free memory is available but scattered in small non-contiguous blocks, too small for new segments. Solutions: 1) Compaction - move segments to consolidate free space (expensive). 2) Best/First fit allocation algorithms. 3) Paged segmentation - divide segments into fixed pages, eliminating external fragmentation.
